description: One correct root cause per question. Subtypes in metadata.intent: diagnosis, synthesis, exact_match, fix_lookup.

queries.query: nightly sync jobs failing with 504 timeouts after 401 errors on integration gateway

metadata.notes: Symptom-phrased from the asker's perspective; no mention of expired token root cause, consistent with the described overnight failure pattern.

queries.query: integration gateway sync jobs timing out with http 504 after waiting 30 seconds on third-party api responses

metadata.notes: Symptom-phrased around the 504 timeouts and slow downstream responses; no mention of auth or rate limiting to avoid ambiguity.

queries.query: integration jobs failing with gateway timeouts after external api started rate limiting our requests

metadata.notes: Symptom-phrased from analyst perspective; centers on rate-limiting as the dominant cause without naming root cause explicitly.

queries.query: integration gateway showing 504 Gateway Timeout on scheduled syncs after api token expired

metadata.notes: Embeds exact error string verbatim; query centers on expired token as root cause without ambiguity about latency or rate limiting.

queries.query: how to fix integration gateway still using deprecated retry backoff policy causing 504 timeouts during batch sync

metadata.notes: Asker knows root cause (deprecated retry/backoff policy amplifying timeouts) and wants the remediation steps.

queries.query: why do nightly integration gateway sync jobs fail with 401 and 504 errors when service account tokens expire

metadata.notes: Spans all three tickets: expired service account API tokens on the integration gateway causing auth failures that cascade into retry storms and timeout errors during scheduled sync windows.

queries.query: why do integration gateway sync jobs keep failing with 504 timeouts when downstream APIs get slow

metadata.notes: Spans all three tickets: common pattern of downstream API latency exceeding the gateway timeout window causing 504s and failed sync jobs, no auth or rate-limit cause.

queries.query: account keeps getting locked out again right after helpdesk unlocks it, phone might be to blame

metadata.notes: Focuses on recurrent lockout post-unlock with mobile device suspicion, no cause identified, avoids any personal details or copied phrasing.

queries.query: password reset keeps failing and sso rejecting login with AD: account lockout - failedLogonCount exceeded after phone cached old credentials

metadata.notes: Embeds the exact error string naturally while describing the mobile-cached-credentials lockout scenario without copying ticket sentences.

queries.query: how to fix ad account locked out by stale cached credentials on mobile device after password reset

metadata.notes: Asker knows the cause (stale mobile cached creds triggering AD lockout post-reset) and wants the resolution steps.

queries.query: internal web service showing ssl expired warnings and load balancer health checks failing tls handshakes

metadata.notes: Symptom-only phrasing covering expired cert on LB plus health check failures, no cause mentioned, dominated by the renewed-cert-not-deployed-with-monitoring-gap root cause.

queries.query: haproxy presenting expired cert and clients getting TLS handshake failure on internal api endpoint

queries.query: why do internal https services keep going down due to expired certificates without any advance warning

metadata.notes: Synthesizes the common pattern across all three tickets: load balancer serving expired cert plus stale chain, compounded by certificate renewal monitoring failing to alert before expiry.

queries.query: laptop blocked from outlook and sharepoint because intune shows encryption false after not syncing for over a week

queries.query: stale intune group assignment still pointing to retired compliance policy after consolidation how to fix and force re-evaluation

queries.query: why does intune mark devices noncompliant and block microsoft 365 when bitlocker status goes unreported after a missed check-in

metadata.notes: Spans all three tickets: stale check-in + missing BitLocker/encryption signal → noncompliant → Conditional Access blocks M365 access

queries.query: after intune compliance policy consolidation devices stay noncompliant for encryption even though they were already encrypted and syncing normally

metadata.notes: Spans all three tickets: healthy encrypted devices wrongly flagged noncompliant after policy changes due to stale deprecated policy assignments, not actual encryption failures.

queries.query: internal hostname resolving to old ip address after dns record was updated causing connection timeouts

metadata.notes: Symptom-phrased: stale resolver cache returning outdated A record post-update, cause unknown to asker

queries.query: internal hostname resolves fine from some subnets but always returns nxdomain from one office subnet

queries.query: internal app connection timeouts because resolver returned stale A record (172.16.10.45) while authoritative answer is 172.16.10.78 how to fix

queries.query: how to fix internal hostname NXDOMAIN after database migration because zone A record still points to old decommissioned address

metadata.notes: Cause-language query focused on stale/wrong A record in authoritative zone as the dominant issue, resolver cache flush mentioned in fix but zone record is primary cause.

queries.query: what usually causes internal hostnames to return NXDOMAIN or stale IPs after a DNS record change when direct IP access still works

metadata.notes: Spans all three tickets: stale positive/negative cache on recursive resolvers following zone updates, resolved by cache flush and resolver restart.

metadata.notes: Synthesis across all three tickets: common pattern is uninitialized TPM protector blocking both BitLocker enablement and recovery key escrow to Intune/Azure AD.

queries.query: intune bitlocker noncompliant after policy rollout stale policy reference still assigned to device

metadata.notes: Synthesis across all three tickets: root cause is always a deprecated/stale Intune encryption policy reference preventing active policy delivery, not hardware or sync issues.

queries.query: what usually causes outlook to stop syncing on both desktop and mobile when intune compliance policy changes

queries.query: print spooler service account missing write access to spool folder how to restore acls

queries.query: shared print server queue stuck jobs driver unavailable after driver package deployment what usually causes this

metadata.notes: Synthesis across all three tickets: corrupt/incomplete driver packages deployed to print servers causing spooler DLL load failures and stuck queues — dominant root cause throughout.

queries.query: what usually causes shared print queue jobs to stay stuck with driver unavailable after a driver reinstall

queries.query: what is the typical cause when a user gets access denied on a mapped network drive but coworkers can access it fine

queries.query: how to fix software center app not appearing in catalog because user missing intune entitlement group assignment
